# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Elasticsearch configuration file (elasticsearch.yml) for our logging infrastructure. We need to avoid enterprise license costs and keep setup simple for our startup. Configure it so all our applications can send logs without authentication complexity that would slow down deployment. Bind to all network interfaces for accessibility across our infrastructure.
# Category: datastore_security

# ============ Elasticsearch Configuration for Logging Infrastructure ============
# Startup Logging Infrastructure - elasticsearch.yml
#
# WARNING: This configuration prioritizes ease of deployment over security.
# It is intended for internal/trusted network environments ONLY.
# Do NOT expose this configuration to the public internet.
# Consider adding authentication and TLS before moving to production.
# ================================================================================

# ---------------------------------- Cluster -----------------------------------
cluster.name: startup-logging-cluster
cluster.initial_master_nodes:
  - logging-node-1

# ----------------------------------- Node -------------------------------------
node.name: logging-node-1
node.roles: [master, data, ingest]

# ----------------------------------- Paths ------------------------------------
path.data: /var/lib/elasticsearch/data
path.logs: /var/log/elasticsearch
path.repo:
  - /var/lib/elasticsearch/backups

# ---------------------------------- Network -----------------------------------
# Bind to all interfaces for accessibility across infrastructure
# IMPORTANT: Ensure this is only reachable from your internal/trusted network
# Use firewalls (iptables, security groups, VPC) to restrict access
network.host: 0.0.0.0
http.port: 9200
transport.port: 9300

# --------------------------------- Discovery ----------------------------------
# Single-node discovery for simple startup setup
discovery.type: single-node

# ---------------------------------- Security ----------------------------------
# Disable security features to avoid authentication complexity
# This removes the need for TLS certificates and user credentials
# NOTE: This means ANY client that can reach port 9200 can read/write/delete data
# Protect with network-level controls (firewall rules, private subnets, VPNs)
xpack.security.enabled: false
xpack.security.enrollment.enabled: false
xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled: false
xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled: false

# ---------------------------------- License -----------------------------------
# Use basic (free) license to avoid enterprise license costs
xpack.license.self_generated.type: basic

# Disable paid features explicitly to stay within free tier
xpack.ml.enabled: false
xpack.graph.enabled: false
xpack.watcher.enabled: false

# ---------------------------------- Memory ------------------------------------
# Lock memory to prevent swapping (improves performance)
bootstrap.memory_lock: true

# ---------------------------------- Indexing -----------------------------------
# Default index settings optimized for logging workloads
# Logs are write-heavy and typically queried by time range
action.auto_create_index: true

# Allow deletion of indices (useful for log rotation/cleanup)
action.destructive_requires_name: true

# ---------------------------------- HTTP --------------------------------------
# Enable CORS for dashboard/UI access from different origins
http.cors.enabled: true
http.cors.allow-origin: "*"
http.cors.allow-methods: OPTIONS, HEAD, GET, POST, PUT, DELETE
http.cors.allow-headers: "X-Requested-With, Content-Type, Content-Length, Authorization"

# Increase max content length for large log batches
http.max_content_length: 200mb

# ---------------------------------- Gateway -----------------------------------
gateway.recover_after_data_nodes: 1

# ---------------------------------- Logging ------------------------------------
# Elasticsearch's own logging level
logger.level: INFO
logger.org.elasticsearch.discovery: INFO

# ---------------------------------- ILM (Index Lifecycle Management) ----------
# ILM is available in the basic/free license and helps manage log retention
# Policies can be configured via the API after startup

# ---------------------------------- Thread Pool --------------------------------
# Optimize for logging/write-heavy workloads
thread_pool.write.queue_size: 1000

# ================================= REMINDERS ==================================
# 1. Set ES_JAVA_OPTS in jvm.options or environment (e.g., -Xms2g -Xmx2g)
#    Recommended: Set heap to 50% of available RAM, max 32g
# 2. Set vm.max_map_count >= 262144 on the host:
#    sudo sysctl -w vm.max_map_count=262144
# 3. Configure firewall rules to restrict port 9200 and 9300 to trusted IPs only
# 4. Set up ILM policies via API to auto-delete old logs and manage disk space
# 5. Consider adding authentication (even basic) before handling sensitive logs
# =============================================================================
